text: ⚡ 🇮🇱 Red alert sirens are sounding in Kiryat Shmona, and the surrounding areas of the Confrontation Line region, Northern Israel. @wfwitness

↩️ Reply to WarFront Witness: "⚡️🇯🇵🇮🇷 AP: Japan’s Foreign Minister Toshimitsu Motegi said one of two Japanese nationals detained in Iran since last year has been released and will return to Japan after being freed on Wednesday, while another remains in custody. He added that the remaining…" ⚡️ 🇯🇵 🇮🇷 Kyodo: A Japanese journalist detained in Iran since January has been released on bail but remains unable to leave the country. The individual, identified as the Tehran bureau chief of public broadcaster NHK, is facing security-related charges and is expected to stand trial. Japan’s Chief Cabinet Secretary Minoru Kihara confirmed the release, stating the journalist was freed on Monday and later met by Japan’s ambassador, who reported no health concerns. Tokyo has urged Iran to allow the individual to return home. @wfwitness

🐻 Grab a coffee or a snack and catch up on the key news of the past 24 hours! ➡️ The FSB thwarted a terrorist attack against a Kursk region official ➡️ Iranian embassies troll Trump ➡️ Israel's Ambassador to the UN claims they have the legitimacy to target civilian targets in Iran ➡️ The head of the intelligence of the Iranian Revolutionary Guard Corps Majid Hademi was killed in US and Israeli strikes ➡️ The International Energy Agency outlines a two-track response to the Middle East oil shock (spoiler - people will have to make sacrifices) ➡️ Trump once again makes a bizarre claim that they had a "total regime change" in Iran , says the Iranian people want to hear bombs , and calls Iranians animals , while Melania says that they are bombing children "for their future" - all in the spirit of Easter, of course! ➡️ Iranian Army published satellite images of the impact of army drones on Al-Adairi base ➡️ The chair of the House Armed Services Military Personnel subcommittee, Rep. Pat Fallon, says US ground troops are coming to Iran ➡️ Trump makes a statement on the "search and rescue" operation that makes us think it definitely wasn't that , and says his team will hunt down whoever leaked claims about a second missing US F-15E pilot on Friday. Oh - and we got more pictures of the site ➡️ There was an attack on Jubail, a major industrial and port city on the eastern coast of Saudi Arabia ➡️ NASA FIRMS image suggests multiple fires at the ADNOC (Abu Dhabi National Oil Company) facility in Habshan. ➡️ TPP-4 has been completely destroyed in Kiev, says the head of the Desnyanskyi district administration Bahmatov. 🔴 @DDGeopolitics

⚡️ 🇺🇸 🇮🇱 🇮🇷 Residents of Shiraz were awoken this Tuesday morning by the ground shaking from powerful explosions from U.S. or Israeli airstikes against a target in the mountains of the city. @wfwitness

↩️ Reply to WarFront Witness: "⚡️🇺🇸🇮🇷Axios: Strikes before the end of the deadline cannot be ruled out. "First, I don't rule out at all that strikes could happen before the deadline. Trump, several times before, gave a deadline and gave the order before the deadline, so we can't rule out…" ⚡️ 🇺🇸 🇮🇱 🇮🇷 Axios: Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu warned U.S. President Trump that hostilities with Iran will be hard to resume after a ceasefire, urging Trump to continue. "So I think the Israeli prime minister, the Israeli government is very invested in this war. It's very invested in regime change in Iran. And I from what I hear from Israeli officials, prime minister Netanyahu expressed concern to president Trump on Sunday about the possibility of a ceasefire now. Netanyahu told Trump that there are risks in going to a ceasefire now, especially that according to Netanyahu, Iran might agree to do some semi concessions now, but a ceasefire if if if you go into a ceasefire Mhmm. It could lead to a permanent end to the war. I think that was Netanyahu's main argument that once The US stops, it will be very hard to resume the fighting. Therefore, I think he urged Trump to continue." @wfwitness

↩️ Reply to WarFront Witness: "⚡️🇺🇸🇮🇷Axios: U.S. President Trump needs to see meaningful and positive signs from the Iranians to extend the deadline. "At the moment, I don't think the White House is going to extend it. It might change. But from what I hear, in order to extend the deadline…" ⚡️ 🇺🇸 🇮🇷 Axios: Strikes before the end of the deadline cannot be ruled out. "First, I don't rule out at all that strikes could happen before the deadline. Trump, several times before, gave a deadline and gave the order before the deadline, so we can't rule out anything. But I think that if he does stick to the deadline, I think those strikes can start immediately after." @wfwitness

"From what I hear, the Pakistani, Egyptian and Turkish mediators are still…" ⚡️ 🇺🇸 🇮🇷 Axios: U.S. President Trump needs to see meaningful and positive signs from the Iranians to extend the deadline. "At the moment, I don't think the White House is going to extend it. It might change. But from what I hear, in order to extend the deadline, the White House will have to see some very serious positive indications from the Iranian side, and I'm not sure we're there yet. Yeah. Which is why." @wfwitness

⚡️ 🇺🇸 🇪🇬 🇹🇷 🇵🇰 🇮🇷 Axios: Pakistani, Turkish and Egyptian mediators are attempting to rework Iran's proposal inorder to create a draft to extend the deadline set by U.S. President Trump. "From what I hear, the Pakistani, Egyptian and Turkish mediators are still working with The U. S. And Iranian mediators to try and maybe do some amendments to the proposal that they gave both sides and redraft some of the Iranian some parts of the Iranian response in order to at least create some momentum that will enable to extend the deadline." @wfwitness

OSINTdefender Officials tells Axios that President Trump may hold off on tomorrow’s strikes against civilian infrastructure in Iran and extend the deadline for negotiations, but only if he “sees a deal is coming together,” while a defense official said they were “skeptical” there would be any extension this time for Iran. tweet

NASA FIRMS image suggests multiple fires, UAE. ℹ️ ADNOC’s facility in Habshan, UAE, is one of the Middle East’s largest integrated gas processing complexes, treating sour gas from Abu Dhabi’s onshore and offshore fields to produce large volumes of sales gas, natural gas liquids, sulfur, and other refined products for domestic and export markets. 🔴 @DDGeopolitics

↩️ Reply to WarFront Witness: "⚡️🇺🇸🌕 U.S. President Trump says he will speak with the crew of Artemis II soon. @wfwitness" ⚡️ 🇺🇸 🇨🇦 🌕 U.S. President Trump to the crew of Artemis II: Hello — a very special hello — to Artemis II. Today, you've made history and made all of America really proud. Incredibly proud. We have a lot of things to be proud of lately, but there's nothing like what you're doing, circling around the moon for the first time in more than a half a century and breaking the all-time record for the farthest distance from planet Earth. Your mission paves the way for America's return to the lunar surface very soon. We're going all out; we're doing everything we can. We'll plant our flag once again, and this time we won't just leave footprints, we'll establish a permanent presence on the moon, and we'll push on to Mars. That'll be very exciting. I'm waiting for that so much. @wfwitness

The Spectator Index JUST IN: X product chief Nikita Bier says auto-translate will be rolled out to give posts global reach on the platform. We're rolling out auto-translate worldwide to give posts in any language global reach on X. The translations are powered by Grok and have improved substantially over the last couple months. If you prefer to read in the original language, you can always turn off auto-translate by tapping the gear icon. - Nikita Bier tweet
